title: Monetary Policy Transmission in Nonclearing Labor & Capital Markets
abstract: This paper investigates the supply-side transmission channels of monetary policy when input and labor markets do not instantaneously clear. Using a quantitative New Keynesian framework augmented with market search frictions, I show that interest rate shocks induce asymmetric real wage and price adjustments.

title: Deep Learning for High-Dimensional Macrofinance and Monetary DSGE Models
abstract: We propose a neural network architecture to solve non-linear dynamic stochastic general equilibrium (DSGE) models with heterogeneous agents and aggregate shocks. The method overcomes the curse of dimensionality and facilitates real-time policy evaluation.
